TEST.0.title: Codebusters 2026 Division B Regional Test # 2
TEST.0.customHeader:
Names of participants: (Please print neatly)

 
 
 
School Name:

Check your team:  ◻ Varsity     ◻ JV1     ◻ JV2     ◻ JV3

Warning: Do not open this packet until given permission to do so.

Note: There are useful notes after this page.

CIPHER.315.cipherString: One could not always put safety up front as the prime goal. Do that, and who would ever achieve anything of note?
CIPHER.315.question: Jack McDevitt offers an observation that has been encoded as an Aristocrat. What does it say?
CIPHER.315.author: Jack McDevitt

CIPHER.299.cipherString: Failure implies a failed day not a failed life.
CIPHER.299.question: Your graphing calculator won’t stop displaying this Fractionated Morse puzzle by Amit Kalantri. You are told that the quote has DDAYN in it corresponding to the encoded text C Z I E D O F, meaning C = –●●; D = –●–; E = ●–×; F = –●×; I = ●●×; O = –××; Z = ××–. What does it say?
CIPHER.299.author: Amit Kalantri

CIPHER.293.cipherString: Long ago I discovered that there was more to life than cricket, and more to cricket than runs and wickets.
CIPHER.293.question: David Foot offers us some advice that has been encoded as an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher starts with LONG. What does it say?
CIPHER.293.author: David Foot

CIPHER.313.cipherString: Those who lack the courage will always find a philosophy to justify it.
CIPHER.313.question: Projected by a broken laser pointer during physics class, solve this Checkerboard quote from Albert Camus with a polybius key of PITCHERS. What does it say?
CIPHER.313.author: Albert Camus

CIPHER.291.cipherString: Bunsen was no theorist, and purely speculative questions had little or no interest for him.
CIPHER.291.question: A riddle is discovered taped under a chemistry lab bench. It's a quote, encrypted as an Aristocrat. What does it say?

CIPHER.309.cipherString: If you look at nature closely, you will notice miracles all around you.
CIPHER.309.question: During a field trip, you notice these strange markings on a metal plaque. They represent a quote by Shree Shambav, encoded using an Atbash Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.309.author: Shree Shambav

CIPHER.306.cipherString: Fear of danger is ten thousand times more terrifying than danger itself.
CIPHER.306.question: An explorer's journal mentions a “hidden truth” encoded below. It turns out to be a quote by Daniel Defoe, written in a Complete Columnar Cipher. The quote has ORETERR somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.306.author: Daniel Defoe

CIPHER.292.cipherString: Pain reaches the heart with electrical speed, but truth moves to the heart as slowly as a glacier.
CIPHER.292.question: Someone left a paper with the message below on the copy machine in the office. It's a quote by Barbara Kingsolver, encoded as an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.292.author: Barbara Kingsolver

CIPHER.297.cipherString: It's not enough to do good. You have to be seen doing good.
CIPHER.297.question: You open a forgotten email draft saved in the school's computer system. It holds a quote by Rene Balcer encoded in a Baconian. What does it say?
CIPHER.297.author: Rene Balcer

CIPHER.295.cipherString: El problema con ella es que carece de la capacidad de conversación, pero no de la capacidad de hablar.
CIPHER.295.question: A coded message is discovered inside a fortune cookie. It turns out to be a quote by George Bernard Shaw in Spanish, encoded using a Xenocrypt Cipher using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains ELLA. What does it say?
CIPHER.295.author: George Bernard Shaw
CIPHER.295.translation: The trouble with her is that she lacks the power of conversation but not the power of speech.

CIPHER.298.cipherString: Night had come on like the closing of a great but gentle eye.
CIPHER.298.question: The following odd symbols were found when a tomb was opened, but you recognized it as the handiwork of a prankster who scratched a quote by Ray Bradbury on the wall using a Baconian alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.298.author: Ray Bradbury

CIPHER.304.cipherString: We are the masters at the moment and shall be for some considerable time.
CIPHER.304.question: Your robot vacuum just projected this message onto the ceiling. It's a quote by Hartley Shawcross encoded with a Porta using a keyword of VEINS. What does it say?
CIPHER.304.author: Hartley Shawcross

CIPHER.308.cipherString: When I don't understand, I have an unbearable itch to know why.
CIPHER.308.question: A time-capsule USB drive from 1983 just auto-opened with this Nihilist Substitution-encrypted message by Robert A. Heinlein with a keyword of TRIPLE and polybius key of FACTORIES. What does it say?
CIPHER.308.author: Robert A. Heinlein

CIPHER.300.cipherString: Life is too short. I don't have time to speak slowly.
CIPHER.300.question: A glitchy hologram in the school planetarium displays this Fractionated Morse version of Dev Anand's words. You are told that the quote has THAVET in it corresponding to the encoded text Z Y D R Q I A K, meaning A = ●××; D = ●●●; I = ●–×; K = –×●; Q = ×●●; R = ×●–; Y = ××●; Z = ××–. What does it say?
CIPHER.300.author: Dev Anand

CIPHER.312.question: Etched into the surface of a melted vinyl record, you found this Affine-encrypted quote by Alfred North Whitehead with a=11 and b=6. What does it say?
CIPHER.312.author: Alfred North Whitehead

CIPHER.294.cipherString: Economists are evaluated on how intelligent they sound, not on a scientific measure of their knowledge of reality.
CIPHER.294.question: You discover a scrap of paper tucked inside an old lab notebook. It contains a quote by Nassim Nicholas Taleb encoded as a Patristocrat using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher ends with ALITY. What does it say?
CIPHER.294.author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb

CIPHER.305.cipherString: One thing is certain in business. You and everyone around you will make mistakes.
CIPHER.305.question: Someone passed you a piece of paper with this Complete Columnar encoded quote by Richard Branson. The quote has AINI somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.305.author: Richard Branson

CIPHER.311.question: Your solar-powered calculator started flashing this Affine sequence—it's Chris Guillebeau's words with a=11 and b=23. What does it say?
CIPHER.311.author: Chris Guillebeau

CIPHER.314.cipherString: If you intend to change, decide what you want and live your life accordingly.
CIPHER.314.question: Your lava lamp started bubbling in Checkerboard code by Steve Maraboli with a polybius key of VANISHED. What does it say?
CIPHER.314.author: Steve Maraboli

CIPHER.307.cipherString: Any tomorrow that will count must start from today.
CIPHER.307.question: A mysterious app update added this Nihilist Substitution lock screen—it's actually Bamigboye Olurotimi's quote with a keyword of AVOID and polybius key of MASCULINE. What does it say?
CIPHER.307.author: Bamigboye Olurotimi

CIPHER.310.cipherString: That which is striking and beautiful is not always good, but that which is good is always beautiful.
CIPHER.310.question: You receive this cryptic message from the future through an online time capsule. It's a quote by Ninon de L'Enclos, written in a Caesar. What does it say?
CIPHER.310.author: Ninon de L'Enclos

CIPHER.301.question: A coded message appears after you shine a UV light on a classroom poster. It turns out to be a quote, encrypted using a Cryptarithm. What do the values 831 735904 2998356256 decode to?

CIPHER.303.cipherString: The technicians thought I was crazy. Now, five months later, I've proved it.
CIPHER.303.question: Found on a microchip inside a disassembled robotics kit: decode this Porta Cipher by James Blish using a keyword of DRUM. What does it say?
CIPHER.303.author: James Blish

CIPHER.302.question: An old robot prototype suddenly prints this encoded text. It's encrypted using a Cryptarithm. What do the values 041956 37241 95841 decode to?

CIPHER.296.cipherString: It is delivery that makes the orator's success.
CIPHER.296.question: A laser-etched message on the inside of a geodes crystal reads: solve this Baconian version of Goethe's wisdom. You are told that the deciphered text starts with ITIS. What does it say?
CIPHER.296.author: Goethe
